sources.name: Legal Aid Agency
sources.dataset: Legal Aid Statistics: England and Wales

metadata.methodology: Legal aid expenditure in real terms (2010 prices) using HM Treasury GDP deflator. Firm counts from Legal Aid Agency provider directories. A legal aid desert is defined as a local authority area where no solicitor firm holds a legal aid contract within 20 miles.
knownIssues: LASPO 2012 (Legal Aid, Sentencing and Punishment of Offenders Act) removed whole categories from legal aid scope; expenditure reductions partly reflect scope reduction rather than rate cuts alone
Firm counts may overstate provision: some contract holders have very limited capacity
Legal aid desert definition varies across sources; Law Society uses different distance thresholds for different specialisms
